title: catinga oblongifolia
intro: Catinga oblongifolia, also known as Quina, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it a valuable resource in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Catinga oblongifolia include its potential to lower fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and even exhibit antimalarial properties. Herbal preparations of Catinga oblongifolia are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to treat a range of ailments, from common colds to more serious conditions like malaria and fever.
